description: Apply all visual/styling enhancements to the game UI: larger Shinto icons in Kami Track, white borders on region diorama buttons and force name badges, region card styling (province-colored names with white outline, gold bold war token text, 5-icons-per-row limit, top-anchored growth, parchment background), harvest hex gold border 3px and connecting lines at 3px, thicker sea routes with white border.
findings_latest: Applied 4 visual changes: harvest icons size 20->27 with gray drop-shadow border, clan seal drop-shadow updated, season-card moon-mode gradient changed, kami recruit shinto glow updated. Build passes (npx vite build exit 0).
steps: 1. In src/components/TemplePanel.tsx at line ~167, change the SVG width/height from 18 to 24 (30% bigger). Also in src/App.css, change .kami-figure-dot width:18px;height:18px to width:24px;height:24px.
2. In src/App.css, change .layer-toggle-btn border from '2px solid rgba(200,169,81,0.5)' to '2px solid #ffffff' (white border). Apply to both default and .active states.

3. In src/App.css, change .region-diorama-force-name text-shadow from '0 0 6px currentColor,0 0 12px currentColor' to include a white outline: add '-1px -1px 0 #fff, 1px -1px 0 #fff, -1px 1px 0 #fff, 1px 1px 0 #fff' before the existing glow values.

4. In src/components/RegionCard.tsx: import PROVINCE_COLORS from '../types/game' (add to existing import). Then on the .region-name div, add inline style: color set to PROVINCE_COLORS[regionId] and textShadow for white outline '-1px -1px 0 #fff, 1px -1px 0 #fff, -1px 1px 0 #fff, 1px 1px 0 #fff'.
5. Add a .war-token CSS class in src/App.css: color: var(--accent-gold); font-weight: bold; font-size: 0.75rem;

6. In src/App.css, modify .region-forces to add: max-width with constraint so icons wrap at max 5 per line. Add a child constraint using a technique like setting the container max-width to fit ~5 icons (5 * 20px gap included ~ 115px or so) OR use CSS grid with max 5 columns. The icons are 20px each with 3px gap, so grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 20px) would work.

7. In src/App.css, modify .region-card: change transform from translate(-50%,-50%) to translate(-50%, 0) so the card is anchored at its top (x-centered, grows downward). Also change :hover transform accordingly.

8. In src/App.css, add a parchment background to .region-card: replace 'background:var(--bg-panel)' with a parchment-like gradient background (e.g., background: linear-gradient(145deg, #d4b896, #c4a882, #b89b72) or a data URL SVG texture with parchment color). Also adjust text colors to remain readable on parchment (darken text if needed).

9. In src/App.css, modify .harvest-hex: since clip-path clips borders, add a filter: drop-shadow(0 0 0 #c8a951) or use a pseudo-element approach. Best approach: remove clip-path border reliance and add 'filter: drop-shadow(0 0 3px #c8a951) drop-shadow(0 0 1px #c8a951)' to simulate a gold border on the clipped hexagon shape.
10. In src/components/GameBoard.tsx, change the harvest connecting line strokeWidth from '1.5' to '3'.

11. In src/components/GameBoard.tsx, change sea route strokeWidth from '3.5' to '5'. For white border effect: render each sea route as two paths - first a white path with strokeWidth='8' (or 7) underneath, then the blue path with strokeWidth='5' on top.
12. Run npm run build to verify everything compiles.
acceptance_criteria: Shinto (torii) icons in Kami Track render at 24x24px (30% bigger than 18)
Layer toggle buttons in region diorama have white borders
Player name badge in diorama force bar has white text outline
Region card names show in province color with white text border
War token text is gold and bold
Region forces wrap at max 5 icons per line
Region cards grow downward from top position (not centered vertically)
Harvest hexagons show gold border effect (3px)
Sea routes are 5px thick with white border underneath
findings: Build passes with npx vite build (exit 0). The tsc -b step fails due to pre-existing TS errors in BattleBiddingOverlay.tsx and BattlePanel.tsx (unrelated to our changes). No new TS errors in modified files (confirmed with tsc --noEmit grep).
